title: Perfoliata gigantea
taxonomy: Perfoliata gigantea is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Perfoliata and the class Magnoliopsida. Further classification places it in the order Asterales, the suborder Asterae, the superorder Asteranae, and the division Magnoliophyta. The family Asteraceae is also known as the daisy or sunflower family, to which many common flowering plants belong. This classification helps understand the relationships and evolutionary history of Perfoliata gigantea and other related species within the plant kingdom.
uses: Perfoliata gigantea, also known as the giant perfoliate plant, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems are believed to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat skin irritations and wounds. In some traditional medicine practices, the plant is used to treat fever and rheumatic conditions.
